violations.text: There was no one food safety certified at time of inspection. The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and floor mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed/sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026

Cut tomatoes and meatballs were observed held between 44-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
Establishment has not been approved to use Time as a Public Health Control for pizza. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
Various items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
The following were observed in poor repair: - Dough prep table (laminate peeling) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Foil is used in the construction of shelf liners in the pizza prep unit and cooking area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: - in between fryer/grill areas - racks in walk-in cooler Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
Inspector observed an accumulation of trash, food, and other debris around dumpster area. Area to be kept clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
The ceiling in the kithen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
The ceilings in the dining area is in poor repair (peeling paint). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026

There was no one food safety certified at time of inspection. The establishment must have a on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Raw animal foods (such as raw beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and floor mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took units out of service to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC informed to call for service. PIC manually added chlorine. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Meatballs was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC dicarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
Establishment has not been approved to use Time as a Public Health Control for pizza. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler and freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
The following was observed in poor repair: - Dough prep table (laminate peeling) - plastic food containers (cracked) New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026

Foil and paper are used in the construction of shelf liners in the dry storage areas and prep unit. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the fryer/grill area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/06/2026
The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
The ceiling in the dining area is in poor repair (peeling). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026
Bowl was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
The handwashing sink in the kitchen did not have running water. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (romaine lettuce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Various food product was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Inspector observed dead rodent on trap in kitchen area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies.PIC removed from premises. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
Boxes of tomato sauce were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and walk in cooler area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the food storage shelves and non food contact surface of the dough mixer in the kitchen area have an accumulation of food residue, debris, and a small accumulation of rodent droppings New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The floors and walls throughout the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and grease buildup. The floors near water heater observed with an accumulation of white foam debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
